channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: “This Is Criminal Neglect of Evidence” — Tobacco Control, Ideology & Harm Reduction Explained
item.description: Tobacco control fails when ideology is allowed to override evidence. Long-term data from Sweden and other countries show that smoke-free nicotine alternatives can help reduce smoking-related harm, yet these outcomes are still too often ignored. Harm reduction does not replace traditional tobacco control — it strengthens it by offering practical routes away from combustible cigarettes. Public health policy should follow real-world evidence, not institutional resistance. (Published on June 11, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)

item.title: Media Watch: The Telegraph is Confused About “Illegal” Disposable Vapes
item.description: Despite the UK's disposable vape ban, a significant number of vapers still use such devices legally. The ban aimed to reduce environmental waste but inadvertently led to challenges for users and manufacturers. Despite efforts to comply with regulations, manufacturers faced criticism. Calls for a deposit on disposable vapes to encourage recycling have been met with pricing concerns. The focus on "loopholes" overlooks the importance of meeting consumer preferences for user-friendly vaping devices, essential for reducing smoking rates effectively and protecting public health. Addressing unintended consequences of vape regulations requires thoughtful market interventions. (Published on June 11, 2026bydaily-pouch.com)

item.title: Impossible by design: the EU survey on future tobacco and nicotine policy
item.description: The EU published a survey on future tobacco and nicotine regulation, aiming to shape upcoming policies. The survey raises concerns due to its limited scope and lack of consideration for harm reduction strategies or citizen input. It highlights a bias towards a prohibitionist approach and fails to address the diverse needs of the one hundred million tobacco and nicotine users in the EU. The survey lacks genuine consultation and instead aims to push a preset agenda. Eight major issues with the questionnaire are identified. (Published on June 11, 2026byethra.co)
